LAW AND MOTION 302, JUL-20-2023. HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT. RULING - ARGUED. UPON CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S "MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES FROM PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT" IS DENIED. THE MOTION IS BASED ON THE NOTION THAT PLAINTIFF "HAS NOT PLED A VIABLE FRAUD CLAIM." AS RULED ON THE ACCOMPANYING DEMURRER, THE COURT DISAGREES - SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR THIS DATE. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER THAT REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-11-2023 Set For Trial On Sep-03-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-20-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (via Zoom):

Harry Terzian, Esq., Quill & Arrow LLP, (310) 933-4271, for Plaintiff.

Jesse Valencia, Esq., Erskine Law, (949) 777-6032, for Defendant.

RE: Defendant GENERAL MOTORS LLC's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Ruling - ARGUED. Upon conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled. The FAC adequately pleads a claim for fraudulent inducement/concealment. Less specificity is required when, as here, the facts lie more within defendant's knowledge than plaintiffs. (Committee on Children's Television, Inc. v. General Foods Corp. (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197, 217.) Nondisclosure or concealment is actionable fraud when, as here, defendant allegedly has knowledge of facts not known to plaintiff. (LiMandri v. Judkins (1997) 52 Cal.App.4th 326, 336.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the court's tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-20-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (via Zoom):

Harry Terzian, Esq., Quill & Arrow LLP, (310) 933-4271, for Plaintiff.

Jesse Valencia, Esq., Erskine Law, (949) 777-6032, for Defendant.

RE: Defendant GENERAL MOTORS LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

Ruling - ARGUED. Upon conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's "motion to strike punitive damages from plaintiff's first amended complaint" is denied. The motion is based on the notion that plaintiff "has not pled a viable fraud claim." As ruled on the accompanying demurrer, the court disagrees.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order that repeats verbatim the court's tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/RBU)
